entries.description: Read start_session.py, close_session.py, session_state.py, session_events.py, disposition.py, gate_checks.py to map the CLI surface, the on-disk state shape, the events ledger, and the five close-out gates. Verified register_session_start lives at session_state.py:148 and preserves completedSessions[] via compute_effective_completed_sessions; verified DISPOSITION_STATUSES = ('completed', 'failed', 'requires_review') and disposition lives at session-set root.

Created ai_router/tests/e2e/conftest.py with sys.path mutation putting e2e/ on path (so test files import 'from fixtures import ...' bare-style), plus autouse fixture scrubbing AI_ROUTER_ALLOW_FORCE_CLOSE_OUT from the env in every test.

Implemented ai_router/tests/e2e/fixtures.py (~390 LOC). Public API: make_session_set, drive_start_session, drive_close_session, make_disposition, make_activity_log_entry, make_change_log, read_state, read_events, plus HarnessHandle dataclass. Each fixture builds a real git repo with a sibling bare remote in tmpdir, auto-commits/pushes between every step so the close-out gates (working-tree-clean, pushed-to-remote, activity-log-entry, next-orchestrator-present, change-log-fresh) pass naturally. PYTHONPATH injected into subprocess env via _subprocess_env() so 'python -m ai_router.start_session' resolves against the repo checkout without requiring pip install -e .

Wrote ai_router/tests/e2e/test_happy_3session.py: drives a 3-session set through start_session → activity log → disposition → close_session for each N, asserts the full state-invariant bundle at every boundary (currentSession, totalSessions, status/lifecycleState transitions, completedSessions[] preservation across rewrites, startedAt/completedAt, orchestrator block persistence). Also asserts events-ledger history is stable for all sessions 1..n at every boundary, not just the current session (per the verifier-driven strengthening described in step 7).

Wrote ai_router/tests/e2e/test_register_session_start_regression.py: two tests that pin the v0.1.1 completedSessions[]-loss bug shut. Calls register_session_start() directly (no CLI subprocess, no git) on a tmpdir state pre-populated with completedSessions: [1] (and a second test with [1, 2, 3] to guard against an off-by-one fix). Asserts the array survives the rewrite. Per the verifier feedback, this file is intentionally NOT marked e2e — it's narrow and fast and should fire on every pre-commit run.

Registered the 'e2e' marker in pytest.ini (NOT pyproject.toml — the spec.md was imprecise; the project's actual pytest config is in pytest.ini alongside the existing 'failure_injection' marker). Verified marker partitions: 'pytest' → 430 passed + 1 skipped; 'pytest -m e2e' → 1 passed (5.6s); 'pytest -m "not e2e"' → 429 passed + 1 skipped + 1 deselected. 427 baseline preserved with 2 new fast tests added.

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 ($0.32, 9.3k input tokens, 19.8k output tokens, 263s elapsed, no escalation). Verifier returned 1 Major + 2 Minor findings: (Major) happy-path event assertions only checked current session, missing history-stability invariant — fixed by adding _assert_event_history_stable() that checks counts across all sessions 1..n at every boundary; (Minor) post-close assertions missing currentSession/totalSessions/startedAt/orchestrator block — added; (Minor) regression test wrongly marked e2e — unmarked so it runs in the fast pre-commit suite. Verifier's proposed 'assert total event count == 2*n' was reduced to per-type counts + out-of-range session_number check because close_session legitimately emits closeout_requested + verification_completed + closeout_succeeded per close, and pinning the total would couple the test to internal close-out sequencing. All three findings addressed; re-ran suite green.

Read session_lifecycle.py, session_state._flip_state_to_closed, worktree.py classification logic before writing Session 2 tests. Key findings: (1) cancel_session_set writes CANCELLED.md + sets preCancelStatus; restore renames to RESTORED.md and clears preCancelStatus; (2) --force and --manual-verify are mutually incompatible; force uses --reason-file alone; (3) force always sets is_last_session=True so a mid-set force-close still flips to status=complete; (4) worktree.enumerate_worktrees uses git worktree list --porcelain; canonical classification requires path.parent == <repo>-worktrees/ and branch == session-set/<slug>.

Extended ai_router/tests/e2e/fixtures.py with cancel_set(), restore_set(), make_sibling_worktree(), make_additional_set() helpers; updated drive_close_session() to accept force=True (adds --force flag, sets AI_ROUTER_ALLOW_FORCE_CLOSE_OUT=1 in subprocess env, omits --manual-verify) and inject_force_env=False (guard test path: adds --force without env var). Discovered incompatibility between --force and --manual-verify and fixed accordingly.

Wrote all four Session 2 test files: test_cancel_restore_midset.py (4-session set, cancel mid-session-3, restore, complete normally; asserts CANCELLED.md/RESTORED.md presence at each boundary, preCancelStatus lifecycle, final completedSessions=[1,2,3,4]); test_force_close_path.py (guard test: env-var required; forensic test: force-close session 2 of 3 writes forceClosed=true, status=complete, closeout_force_used event); test_worktree_discovery.py (makes real git worktree via git worktree add, asserts enumerate_worktrees returns canonical classification with correct slug/branch_matches_convention, plus session-set walkability); test_multiset_sequential.py (three sets in one repo via make_additional_set, drive A→B→C sequentially, assert boundary isolation after each close). All 7 e2e tests pass; full suite 436 passed + 1 skipped (up from 430+1).

Cross-provider verification via gpt-5-4 in 3 sub-rounds (1249 LOC total split at ~500 LOC per the split-large-bundles memory). Round A ($0.17): Major — _commit_and_push used git add -A which could sweep cross-set changes into wrong commits; fixed by staging only handle.set_dir + .harness-reasons/. Minor — AI_ROUTER_ALLOW_FORCE_CLOSE_OUT could leak from dev shell into guard test; fixed by always popping the var then re-injecting only on positive path. Round B ($0.14): VERIFIED — fixtures.py tail + test_cancel_restore_midset.py both clean. Round C ($0.18): Major — multiset test only checked status field, not full state snapshot; fixed by capturing _read_raw_state() before each set run and asserting exact dict equality after. Minors: guard test didn't assert state unchanged after rejection (fixed); force test used `2 in completedSessions` instead of `== [1, 2]` (fixed); events not pinned to session 2 (fixed with `force_events[0].session_number == 2`); no final-session force test (added test_force_close_final_session). All 8 e2e tests green; full suite 437 passed + 1 skipped.
